title: TLS Termination with Ingress
description:
Add HTTPS to an Ingress by creating a `kubernetes.io/tls` Secret containing a self-signed certificate and referencing it in `spec.tls`.

Deploy a simple web service, create a TLS Secret from a pre-generated self-signed cert/key, and configure the Ingress to terminate TLS for `secure.example.com`. The backend continues to receive plain HTTP.

**Common Pitfalls**: The Secret must be type `kubernetes.io/tls` with keys exactly `tls.crt` and `tls.key`. Using type `Opaque` or wrong key names causes the controller to silently skip TLS. The Secret must be in the same namespace as the Ingress.
steps: Create a Deployment named secure-app using image nginx:alpine
Create a ClusterIP Service named secure-app-service on port 80
Create a Secret named tls-secret of type kubernetes.io/tls with data keys tls.crt and tls.key (use the provided base64-encoded self-signed cert values from the hints)
Create an Ingress named tls-ingress with spec.tls referencing tls-secret for host secure.example.com
Add a rule for host secure.example.com routing / (Prefix) to secure-app-service:80
hints: Use app: secure-app as the Pod label — matchLabels, template labels, and Service selector must all use the same value
Secret type must be kubernetes.io/tls — not Opaque
In spec.tls, hosts lists the hostnames covered and secretName is the Secret name

Use this pre-generated self-signed cert (base64) for tls.crt: 
Test with: curl -k -H 'Host: secure.example.com' https://<ingress-ip>/ (the -k flag skips cert verification for self-signed)

editor_config.initial_code:
# Secure app Deployment
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: secure-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: # TODO: use 'secure-app'
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: # TODO: use 'secure-app'
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: secure-app-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: secure-app
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
# TLS Secret
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: tls-secret
type: kubernetes.io/tls
data:
  tls.crt: # TODO: base64-encoded certificate (see hints)
  tls.key: # TODO: base64-encoded private key (see hints)
---
# Ingress with TLS
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: tls-ingress
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - secure.example.com
    secretName: tls-secret
  rules:
  - host: secure.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: secure-app-service
            port:
              number: 80

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: secure-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: secure-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: secure-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: secure-app-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: secure-app
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 80
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: tls-secret
type: kubernetes.io/tls
data:
  tls.crt: 
  tls.key: LS0tLS1CRUdJTiBFQyBQUklWQVRFIEtFWS0tLS0tCk1IUUNBUUVFSUFLMzBONHNFNVZyVSt3c29pMk9NUWJpSWRsM2g3dXhncDBsU0N1MmlYdUpvQW9HQ0NxR1NNNDkKQXdFSG9XUURZd0JLOHlkQ3g1bVhRN2lCYzU5SE5ISmNyR0hkT3pqK3BTY2lyb1BqWjYwQnBqYmwzaEhMZHl6TQpsNDNHd2NqdE5JZkNpNGtzdVBmTm52T04wK2o=
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: tls-ingress
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - secure.example.com
    secretName: tls-secret
  rules:
  - host: secure.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: secure-app-service
            port:
              number: 80
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/secure-app --timeout=60s
echo "✓ secure-app Deployment is ready"

kubectl get service secure-app-service > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ secure-app-service exists" || { echo "✗ secure-app-service not found"; exit 1; }

# Check TLS Secret type
secret_type=$(kubectl get secret tls-secret -o jsonpath='{.type}')
if [ "$secret_type" = "kubernetes.io/tls" ]; then
  echo "✓ Secret type is kubernetes.io/tls"
else
  echo "✗ Secret type is '$secret_type', expected 'kubernetes.io/tls'"
  exit 1
fi

# Check Secret has tls.crt and tls.key
kubectl get secret tls-secret -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.crt}' | grep -q '.' && echo "✓ tls.crt is present" || { echo "✗ tls.crt missing from Secret"; exit 1; }
kubectl get secret tls-secret -o jsonpath='{.data.tls\.key}' | grep -q '.' && echo "✓ tls.key is present" || { echo "✗ tls.key missing from Secret"; exit 1; }

# Check Ingress
kubectl get ingress tls-ingress > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ tls-ingress exists" || { echo "✗ tls-ingress not found"; exit 1; }

# Check TLS section
tls_secret=$(kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.tls[0].secretName}')
if [ "$tls_secret" = "tls-secret" ]; then
  echo "✓ spec.tls[0].secretName is tls-secret"
else
  echo "✗ spec.tls[0].secretName is '$tls_secret', expected 'tls-secret'"
  exit 1
fi

tls_host=$(kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.tls[0].hosts[0]}')
if [ "$tls_host" = "secure.example.com" ]; then
  echo "✓ TLS covers host secure.example.com"
else
  echo "✗ TLS host is '$tls_host', expected 'secure.example.com'"
  exit 1
fi

# Check rule host
rule_host=$(kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -o jsonpath='{.spec.rules[0].host}')
if [ "$rule_host" = "secure.example.com" ]; then
  echo "✓ Rule host is secure.example.com"
else
  echo "✗ Rule host is '$rule_host', expected 'secure.example.com'"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
